content.plain_english: This week in Year 4 maths, your child is working on Money. The learning focuses on: solve addition and subtraction two-step problems in contexts, deciding which operations and methods to use and why; estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence; and measure and calculate the perimeter of a rectilinear figure (including squares) in centimetres and metres. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: Children use practical measuring equipment — rulers, scales, measuring cylinders, and clocks — to explore measurement in real contexts. They record findings using diagrams and number lines before progressing to written calculations. Problem-solving tasks show how these skills are used every day.
content.home_activity: Give your child a small collection of coins and ask them to make different amounts — can they make 37p in two different ways? At the shops (or in a pretend shop at home), let them work out change from £1 or £2. Handling real money builds confidence quickly.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to solve addition and subtraction two-step problems in contexts, deciding which operations and methods to use and why?
Was it easy or tricky to estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: The smaller unit of British money — 100p = £1.
The main unit of British money.
The money you get back when you pay more than something costs.
The overall amount when prices are added together.
The dot in a money amount separating pounds from pence — e.g. £2.50.

example_questions: Find a common factor of 24 and 36.
content.times_table_tip: Year 4 children should know all their times tables to 12 × 12 by the end of the year — the national Multiplication Tables Check happens in Year 4. Daily 3-minute practice mixing tables in random order is the best preparation.
